pi_items.source: ESA Hubble FITS Liberator 'eagle' dataset URL and common astronomical knowledge of M16
Environment setup scripts (install_astroimagej.sh) download the file to this exact path
Calculated from downloaded 656nmos.fits header CD matrix (CD1_1, CD2_1) yielding ~0.0995 arcsec/px, which is rounded to 0.1 for the task description
Task description explicitly states a scale bar representing 'exactly 60 arcseconds'
Calculated based on 60 arcsec / 0.1 arcsec/pixel = 600 pixels
Target path checked by export_result.sh when assessing task success
Source 656nmos.fits image dimensions are 1600x1600. A minimum width constraint of 800 for the exported PNG is a reasonable verification parameter.
Source 656nmos.fits image dimensions are 1600x1600. A minimum height constraint of 800 for the exported PNG is a reasonable verification parameter.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on a real Hubble Space Telescope FITS image of the Eagle Nebula (M16) downloaded from ESA Hubble FITS Liberator Datasets (656nmos.fits). The CD matrix in the FITS header corresponds to a plate scale of ~0.0995 arcsec/pixel, confirming the task's rounded 0.1 arcsec/pixel value. Using this scale, a 60-arcsecond scale bar spans exactly 600 pixels. The export script verifies that the final false-color image is written to '/home/ga/AstroImages/publication/output/eagle_publication.png', and given the original 1600x1600 pixel resolution, minimum output dimensions of 800x800 are appropriate constraints.
